title: Houses for Rent in Kamm's Corners, Cleveland

metaDescription: Houses for rent near Kamm's Corners, Cleveland's far west side. Rent ranges, transit, and commercial-district access. Local property manager. Reach out today.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent Kamm's Corners cleveland
answerBox: Kamm's Corners is a commercial and residential node within Cleveland's larger West Park district, centered on the intersection of Lorain Avenue and Rocky River Drive. Surrounding houses are mostly early-1900s single-family homes; citywide Cleveland 2-bedroom rents run around $1,100/month per Zumper's July 2026 report.

body: Kamm's Corners is the commercial hub at the intersection of Lorain Avenue and Rocky River Drive on Cleveland's far west side, within the larger West Park statistical planning area. The name refers to both the business district — a strip of shops and restaurants along Lorain Avenue — and the surrounding residential blocks.

Housing around Kamm's Corners consists mostly of early-1900s single-family homes on tree-lined side streets, a housing stock typical of Cleveland's west-side streetcar-suburb era. The district sits close to the city's western border with Fairview Park, and its commercial strip hosts an annual outdoor festival and a seasonal farmers market that draw visitors from nearby west-side neighborhoods.

sections.h2: How much do houses rent for near Kamm's Corners?
body: There's no rent index specific to Kamm's Corners, so the best available benchmark is citywide Cleveland data. Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts the Cleveland median asking rent at $1,250/month overall, with 2-bedroom units averaging around $1,100 and 3-bedroom units around $1,350. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report on professionally-managed apartment buildings shows a higher citywide average of $1,564/month, which reflects newer, larger complexes rather than the older single-family stock typical of this area.

Our own managed portfolio across Greater Cleveland runs roughly $700 to $1,800/month, typically around $1,000, and includes single-family houses, duplexes, triplexes, and fourplexes — mostly 2- and 3-bedroom.

GCRTA bus routes run along Lorain Avenue and Rocky River Drive directly through the Kamm's Corners business district, connecting to downtown Cleveland and neighboring west-side suburbs, per RTA's published route network. The area is also close to I-71, giving drivers a quick route toward downtown or south toward the airport.

items: **Location:** Commercial node at Lorain Ave & Rocky River Dr, within West Park
**Transit:** GCRTA bus lines along Lorain Ave and Rocky River Dr
**Highways:** Near I-71; convenient to Cleveland Hopkins International Airport
**Commercial district:** Shops and restaurants along Lorain Avenue near the Kamm's Corners intersection

sections.h2: What are homes like near Kamm's Corners?
body: Most homes near Kamm's Corners are single-family houses built in the early-to-mid 20th century, typically with front porches, small yards, and basements — consistent with the broader West Park area's housing stock. Lot sizes tend to be modest by suburban standards but larger than the tighter grids found in Cleveland's near-west neighborhoods like Ohio City or Clark-Fulton, since Kamm's Corners developed later as a streetcar suburb rather than a 19th-century urban district. For a citywide comparison across neighborhoods and budgets, see our [Cleveland houses for rent guide](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/), or browse [houses by bedroom count and budget](/cleveland-rentals/houses/).

sections.h2: What should renters know before signing a lease near Kamm's Corners?
body: Ohio law generally requires a landlord to return a tenant's security deposit within 30 days after the lease ends and the tenant moves out and provides a forwarding address, along with an itemized list of any deductions, under Ohio Revised Code 5321.16. If a deposit exceeds $50 or one month's rent (whichever is greater) and the tenant stayed six months or more, the landlord generally owes 5% annual interest on the excess. Renters who feel a deposit was wrongfully withheld may be entitled to recover double the amount plus attorney's fees under the same statute — this is general information, not legal advice, so verify current details with the [Ohio Revised Code](https://codes.ohio.gov/ohio-revised-code/section-5321.16) or a local tenant-rights resource before acting.

Our current inventory leans toward the city's east and southeast side, with fewer homes on the far west side, so specific availability near Kamm's Corners will vary. faq.q: Where exactly is Kamm's Corners in Cleveland?
faq.a: Kamm's Corners is the commercial intersection of Lorain Avenue and Rocky River Drive on Cleveland's far west side, part of the larger West Park statistical planning area near the city's border with Fairview Park.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house near Kamm's Corners?
faq.a: There's no neighborhood-specific rent index, but citywide Cleveland data from Zumper (July 2026) shows 2-bedroom homes averaging around $1,100/month and 3-bedroom homes around $1,350/month. Actual listings near Kamm's Corners vary by size and condition.

faq.a: Acceptance depends on each individual landlord — Ohio has no statewide requirement, and Cleveland does not currently have a source-of-income protection ordinance.
